rationale: This is a classic and simple textbook example of the Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction. N-chloro-N-methylbutylamine undergoes intramolecular 1,5-hydrogen atom transfer upon treatment with acid (often sulfuric acid) and light/heat, followed by cyclization to form N-methylpyrrolidine. This example clearly demonstrates the key transformation: remote C-H functionalization via a nitrogen-centered radical to form a five-membered ring.
collision_avoidance_note: The reaction involves a specific intramolecular C-H amination via a haloamine precursor. It is distinct from intermolecular aminations (e.g., Hofmann rearrangement which involves an amide to amine conversion, or Curtius/Schmidt rearrangements). The acyclic substrate and simple pyrrolidine product avoid motifs common in other named reactions like arene functionalization, ring expansions, or decarboxylations.
